codeReview.summary: Core order-type semantics are incomplete for this feature commit. Limit-order placement scaffolding exists, but IOC/FOK/market behavior does not satisfy the feature's expected fill behavior.

issues.description: IOC orders never attempt matching; they are always fully unlocked/cancelled immediately. This does not meet expected behavior requiring IOC to fill available liquidity and cancel only the remainder.

Market orders do not match against the opposing book; they only lock+unlock funds and emit an event. Expected behavior requires market buy/sell to consume opposing liquidity with no resting remainder.
FOK behavior is incorrect on the success path: after liquidity check, the order falls through to resting insertion (line 192) instead of filling atomically or aborting. FOK must never rest on book.
order_type input is not validated at the contract boundary. Unsupported values can bypass IOC/FOK/PostOnly branches and be inserted as resting orders with invalid order_type state.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature spec is internally inconsistent on sequencing: it says matching is a next feature, but expectedBehavior for this feature requires IOC/FOK/market fill semantics that depend on matching. This ambiguity likely enabled a stubbed implementation to be marked complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json (contract-order-placement): description mentions "attempt matching via match_order (next feature)" while expectedBehavior requires IOC partial fills and market fills.

sharedStateObservations.observation: move-worker skill guidance lacks a test-time note that timestamp::set_time_has_started_for_testing requires a @0x1 signer; worker explicitly hit this and suggested updating the skill.
Skill procedure says "Write tests FIRST (TDD)" but transcript shows implementation and compile iterations before any red-stage failing tests, suggesting either procedure drift or unenforced guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for workerSessionId 50a920f2-a8a4-455e-a01a-2f827474933a shows module creation/compilation before test execution.

summary: Review failed. The commit adds order placement scaffolding and tests, but IOC/FOK/market semantics do not implement the required fill behavior, and order_type boundary validation is missing.
